title: Communication Plan Template
content: Communication plan fields: Stakeholder (name/role); Information Need (what they need to know); Frequency (daily/weekly/monthly/milestone); Channel (email/meeting/dashboard/report); Content (what is communicated); Format (report/slide/verbal); Owner (who sends); Feedback mechanism.

title: RACI Communication Matrix
content: RACI matrix for communication: Responsible (creates/delivers the communication); Accountable (approves before sending); Consulted (provides input/review); Informed (receives the communication). Map communication types to RACI roles. Prevents both under-communication and over-communication.

title: Status Report Template (RAG)
content: Status report structure: Project/Sprint name and date; Overall RAG status (Red/Amber/Green with definition); Accomplishments this period; Planned next period; Risks (description/impact/mitigation/owner); Issues requiring decision; Key decisions made; Actions (owner/due date); Metrics (budget/schedule/scope/quality).

title: RAG Status Definitions
content: RAG status: Green = on track/no significant issues/will deliver as planned. Amber = at risk/issue identified but manageable/recovery plan in place. Red = off track/significant issue/escalation required/stakeholder action needed. Always include what changed from last period and why.
tags: rag status green amber red reporting escalation

title: Stakeholder Presentation Structure
content: Executive presentation structure: Hook (business problem/opportunity — 30 seconds); Current State (pain/cost — 1 minute); Proposed Solution (what/how/why — 2 minutes); Demo (show not tell — 3 minutes); Business Case (ROI/timeline/risk — 1 minute); Ask (decision/approval needed — 30 seconds); Q&A.

title: Requirements Walkthrough Facilitation
content: Walkthrough steps: distribute material 48h before; set agenda (scope/objectives/time limit); roles (author/moderator/reviewers/scribe); walkthrough rules (no solutions just issues); capture issues in issue log (not in document); rate each issue (major/minor/question); no acceptance during walkthrough; schedule follow-up.

title: Sprint Review Presentation Structure
content: Sprint review structure: Sprint goal recap (met/not met); Demo of completed stories (happy path first); What was not completed and why; Metrics (velocity/burndown/defects); Stakeholder feedback collection; Product backlog update preview; Next sprint goal preview; Questions and decisions needed.

title: Steering Committee Report Template
content: Steering committee report: Executive summary (3 bullets max); Project health dashboard (RAG for scope/schedule/budget/quality/risk); Milestone status (completed/upcoming); Critical decisions required (with recommendation and deadline); Top 3 risks with mitigation; Budget vs actuals; Next review date.

title: Escalation Procedure
content: Escalation path: Level 1 (team lead — same day); Level 2 (project manager — within 2 hours for critical); Level 3 (sponsor/steering committee — within 4 hours for showstopper). Escalation message must include: issue description; impact (cost/time/quality); options considered; recommended action; decision needed by.
tags: escalation procedure path critical issue decision

title: Decision Log Template
content: Decision log fields: Decision ID; Date; Decision made; Context/background; Options considered; Decision rationale; Made by (name/role); Approved by; Impact (requirements/design/budget/timeline); Follow-up actions; Status (open/decided/implemented).

title: Risk Register Template
content: Risk register fields: Risk ID; Description; Category (technical/resource/external/project); Probability (1-5); Impact (1-5); Risk Score (P x I); Status (open/mitigated/closed); Mitigation strategy; Contingency plan; Owner; Review date; Trigger (event that indicates risk is materializing).

title: Change Request Communication Template
content: Change request communication: Change ID and title; Requester and date; Description of change; Reason/business justification; Impact analysis (scope/schedule/budget/quality/dependencies); Options (implement as requested/partial/alternative/reject); Recommendation; Approval required from; Decision deadline.

title: Go/No-Go Decision Framework
content: Go/No-Go criteria: define criteria before meeting (not during); each criterion rated Green/Amber/Red; unanimous Green required for Go; any Red = automatic No-Go pending resolution; Amber = risk acceptance by sponsor required; document decision with rationale; assign owner for each Amber/Red item.
tags: go-no-go decision framework launch release criteria

title: Meeting Minutes Template
content: Meeting minutes: Date/time/location; Attendees (name/role/present-absent); Apologies; Agenda items covered; Decisions made (each as numbered item); Actions (action description/owner/due date/status); Parking lot (topics deferred); Next meeting (date/agenda); Distributed to within 24 hours.

title: Requirement Clarification Email Template
content: Clarification email structure: Subject (Project name — Requirement Clarification Needed — REQ-ID); Context sentence (referencing document/section); Specific question (numbered list — max 3 per email); Impact if unanswered (brief); Response needed by (specific date); Offer alternatives (if you can answer partially...).

title: Conflict De-escalation Scripts
content: De-escalation phrases: Acknowledge (I hear that this is frustrating/important to you); Clarify (Help me understand your concern — what specifically is the issue?); Validate (That is a valid concern — I want to make sure we address it); Reframe (Let us focus on what we can control here); Forward (What would a good resolution look like for you?).

title: How to Say No to Stakeholders
content: Saying no effectively: Never say just No; Use Yes-And or Yes-If approach; Yes-And: acknowledge value then explain constraint (Great idea — and to do that we would need to defer X or increase budget by Y); Yes-If: offer conditional acceptance (We can do that if we drop Z from scope); Always offer alternatives.

title: Managing Scope Creep Conversation
content: Scope creep response: Name it (This is a new requirement not in scope); Value it (I can see why this is important); Impact it (Adding this would require N days and $X); Options (we can add to backlog for Phase 2/trade off against Y/raise change request/deprioritize Z); Decision (who decides — document either way).

title: Presenting Trade-offs to C-Level
content: C-level trade-off presentation: Lead with recommendation not options; Frame as business decision (not technical); Use cost-benefit not feature comparison; Quantify impact in business terms (revenue/risk/customer); Two options max (preferred + alternative); State risk of inaction; Request specific decision.

title: Stakeholder Communication Frequency Matrix
content: Communication frequency by stakeholder type: Sponsor (weekly status + immediate escalations); Steering Committee (monthly steering report); Business Owners (bi-weekly requirements review); End Users (sprint demos + UAT sessions); Technical Teams (daily/weekly depending on phase); External Vendors (per contract SLA.

title: Requirement Ambiguity Communication Pattern
content: When ambiguity found: document the ambiguity specifically (not vaguely); identify the impact (what decision is blocked); propose 2-3 interpretations with implications each; request stakeholder to select or clarify; set response deadline; track in open issues log; follow up if no response within agreed window.

title: Stakeholder Resistance Management
content: Resistance management: identify type (fear of change/loss of control/skepticism/political); tailor response to type; use WIIFM (What Is In It For Me) framing; involve resistant stakeholder early as reviewer; give ownership of part of solution; acknowledge past pain points; track sentiment changes over time.

title: Kickoff Meeting Communication Template
content: Kickoff agenda: Welcome and introductions; Project background and business case (10 min); Scope — what is in and out (15 min); Timeline and milestones (10 min); Team structure and roles/RACI (10 min); Communication plan and tools (5 min); Working norms (meetings/availability/decisions) (10 min); Q&A and next steps (10 min).

title: Written Communication Clarity Checklist
content: Written communication checklist: bottom-line up front (BLUF — key message in first sentence); active voice; specific not vague (numbers/dates/names); one topic per email; clear call to action with deadline; recipient list correct (To vs CC); subject line descriptive; attachments named descriptively; proofread before send.

title: Virtual Meeting Facilitation Best Practices
content: Virtual facilitation: send agenda 24h before; use video on for engagement; mute all except speaker; use names when addressing someone; visual aids shared on screen; use polls for decisions; breakout rooms for small group work; record with permission; summarize decisions at end; send minutes within 2 hours.
